action.reasoning: Observation: Latest (iter12) val mean_r2=0.596/r2_q=0.215 but test mean_r2=-1756 (r2_q=-4054) vs iter11's test mean_r2=0.303 (all positive, selected on val_r2_q). Clipping, weighted loss, residual MLP, noise help but gen gap on q persists due to test dist shift (outliers). Analysis: Larger model+mean_r2 selection overfits val q-dynamics; test r2 hypersensitive to few large errors. Smaller capacity, higher reg (dropout/noise/q_weight/output_std), select on val_r2_q, moderate clip will target q robustness. Curves show val plateau, more epochs with cosine LR feasible (~22min). Decision: Set hidden=640/blocks=4/dropout=0.45/noise=0.12/q_weight=18/output_std=5e-5/select_on_r2_q/epochs=45/clip[-40,40]; update all scripts, train, submit_eval on best.pt.
